notes.claim: On July 7, 1937, a shooting incident occurred between Chinese and Chinese forces near the Marco Polo Bridge.
notes.context: The incident occurred when a gunshot erupted during a nighttime exercise by the Japanese military, and one soldier went missing for a while. Opinions differ between Japan and China as to who fired the first shot, and it is still not confirmed historically.
notes.verdict: The incident has been confirmed (the source of the shooting is discussed)

sources.title: Documents related to the Marco Polo Bridge Incident (in Japanese)

notes.claim: The Marco Polo Bridge Incident was the beginning of a full-scale war between Japan and China.
notes.context: After the incident, a temporary ceasefire was established in the area, but the Japanese Konoe Cabinet issued a ``Statement to Dispatch Troops to the North'' and decided to send reinforcements. The situation escalated, and after the Second Shanghai Incident in August, the Sino-Japanese War developed into a full-scale war. In China, this incident is known as the ``77 Incidents'' and is regarded as the starting point of the Anti-Japanese War.

sources.title: History of the Sino-Japanese War (in Japanese)
sources.publisher: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan
